description: Global services (IAM, Route53, CloudFront) fail with SignatureDoesNotMatch when user's profile has a non-us-east-1 default region and no explicit --region is specified. Signing region must come from endpoints.json credentialScope, not user's config region.

verify.expect: Should return Users list, not SignatureDoesNotMatch error
Should return HostedZones list, not SignatureDoesNotMatch error

notes: Found during Session 11 parity testing. Root cause: driver used user's config region for SigV4 signing of global services. Fix: Added resolve_signing_region() that reads credentialScope.region from endpoints.json for non-regionalized services. Applied to both main execute path and handle_wait_command. 8 unit tests.

description: raws outputs timestamps in local timezone (e.g., 2023-01-01T00:00:00-07:00) while AWS CLI v2 outputs in UTC (2023-01-01T07:00:00+00:00). Affects all services with timestamp fields in JSON output.

verify.run: raws iam list-policies --scope Local --output json 2>&1 | grep Date | head -2
verify.expect: Timestamps should end with +00:00 (UTC), not local timezone offset

notes: Found during Session 11 parity testing. Root cause: normalize_timestamp() converted to chrono::Local. AWS CLI v2 uses UTC for JSON API output. Fix: Changed to keep timestamps in UTC (+00:00). Note: s3 ls correctly uses local time for display (different from JSON API output).
